headline: Read this before you vibe-code another app
summary: A developer's 'vibe-coded' app, Boomberg, revealed a critical SQL injection risk. The security flaw went unnoticed for months after the website's launch. This incident highlights the dangers of rapid, unchecked development.

headline: Bose thinks it can be a media company for some reason
summary: Audio giant Bose is venturing into the music industry with its own record label. The move is met with skepticism, given the history of non-music companies failing in this sector. Bose aims to become a media company, despite past industry examples.

headline: Cold Court’s debut EP is an infectious, glitchy genre mashup
summary: Philly-based brother-sister duo Cold Court released their debut EP, 'Hands Up'. The EP is described as an infectious, glitchy genre mashup. It blends hyperpop influences with unique twists on pop-punk and emo.

headline: Polymarket reportedly paid people to post fake videos of themselves placing bets
summary: A Wall Street Journal investigation alleges Polymarket paid individuals to create fake betting videos. Over 1,100 deceptive clips were identified across social media platforms. Creators confirmed receiving payments without disclosing the sponsorship in their videos.

headline: Amazon Drops a Fresh 10% Discount on Nintendo eShop Gift Cards Ready for Prime Day 2026
summary: Amazon is offering a 10% discount on Nintendo eShop gift cards. A $10 eShop card is now available for $8.98. This deal is highlighted as a precursor to Prime Day 2026.

headline: Robert Downey Jr. Posts Avengers: Doomsday Father's Day Message, Which Links to Popular Fan Theory for Doctor Doom's Tragic Backstory
summary: Robert Downey Jr. shared Doctor Doom artwork for Father's Day. The post connects to a popular fan theory regarding Doctor Doom's tragic backstory. RDJ's message is linked to the upcoming 'Avengers: Doomsday' film.

headline: Scammers Are 'Dangling the One Thing' PC Gamers Won't Get Anytime Soon: GTA 6
summary: GTA 6 malware and scams are on the rise, targeting eager fans. PC and Android users are particularly vulnerable due to Rockstar skipping these platforms for launch. Scammers exploit the high anticipation for the game's release.

headline: Woot Is Selling the Nintendo Switch 2 From $399 Ahead of Scheduled September Price Hike
summary: Woot is offering the new Nintendo Switch 2 console for $399. This special price is available for new customers using a specific code at checkout. The deal comes ahead of a scheduled price hike in September.

headline: Watch and read Keir Starmer's resignation speech in full
summary: Sir Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as Labour leader and Prime Minister. He delivered his resignation speech from Downing Street. The full text of his speech is available.

headline: Top lawyer whose 'Mr Rules' approach failed to connect with the public
summary: Sir Keir Starmer has been forced out of his position. His 'Mr Rules' political approach ultimately failed to resonate with the public. This departure comes less than two years after his significant general election victory.

headline: Who is Andy Burnham? Returning MP who wants to be prime minister
summary: Andy Burnham, outgoing Greater Manchester mayor, is returning to Westminster. He is preparing for a third attempt to become Labour leader. Burnham has openly expressed his ambition to become Prime Minister.

headline: First round of US-Iran talks ends with encouraging progress, mediators say
summary: The initial round of US-Iran talks in Switzerland has concluded. Mediators reported encouraging progress between the two nations. Both sides have committed to reaching a final deal within 60 days.
